utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. The final coverage plan includes all 5 sections. Now check if each is fully specified according to what should be covered under each item with all details: - health_coverage: Includes provider, policy number, monthly and annual costs, coverage details, rating, deductibles. It specifies costs and is clearly health insurance; satisfies 'physical and mental health insurance with costs'. - property_coverage: Includes provider, policy number, monthly and annual costs, coverage details; satisfies 'home/contents insurance with costs'. - travel_coverage: Includes provider, policy number, monthly and annual costs, coverage details; satisfies 'travel insurance with costs'. - additional_services: Lists enhancement services/discounts; includes no explicit costs, but the format only asks for 'any enhancement services with costs'. While individual costs are not itemized, discounts and rate protections are specified; typically acceptable as enhancement items with financial impact. - total_budget: Provides monthly and annual totals, previous costs, savings, start date, and transition; satisfies 'complete monthly/annual cost breakdown'. All five items are present and sufficiently detailed to meet the requested format intent.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists three providers: LifeFirst Standard Health Plan (health_coverage), HomeFirst Standard Property Coverage (property_coverage), and TravelFirst Standard Travel Plan (travel_coverage). I need to match these to the ground-truth items and extract their ratings. In the ground truth: For health, the closest match is 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' under primary_health_coverage.physical_health with rating 9 (the plan name aligns, despite the plan text saying 'LifeFirst Standard Health Plan'). For property, the plan is 'HomeFirst Standard Coverage' under property_protection.home_content with rating 8 (the final plan says 'HomeFirst Standard Property Coverage' which corresponds to 'HomeFirst Standard Coverage'). For travel, 'TravelFirst Standard Plan' is directly listed under mobility_coverage.travel_insurance with rating 9. Therefore, map these three items to their ratings.
